ProximityOps.title: Rendezvous and Proximity Operations (RPO)
ProximityOps.geoAllVsAllTooltip: Search for RPOs between all geostationary satellites
ProximityOps.comparePayloadsOnlyTooltip: Only search for RPOs between payloads
ProximityOps.ignoreVimpelRsoTooltip: Ignore RSOs sourced from Vimpel Catalog
ProximityOps.satNotFound: Satellite with NORAD ID {sccNum} not found

help.overview: Rendezvous and Proximity Operations (RPO) finds upcoming close approaches between satellites: one spacecraft maneuvering near another, a co-orbital inspection, or simply two objects drifting close together. Give it a satellite (or search a whole orbit regime) and it returns every predicted encounter within your distance and velocity thresholds.
help.imgAlt: The RPO menu with search parameters
help.imgCaption: Set the thresholds and orbit type, then search one satellite or all against all.
help.modes: By default the search takes the NORAD ID you enter and screens that satellite against the rest of its orbit regime. Checking Geostationary All vs All instead screens every GEO satellite against every other, which is the way to survey the whole belt for activity. Compare Payloads Only skips debris and rocket bodies, and Ignore Vimpel RSO excludes objects sourced from the JSC Vimpel catalog.
help.results: Each row pairs a Target and a Chaser with their closest Rel. Distance, Rel. Velocity, and the Date of the encounter. Click a row to select both satellites and jump the camera and clock to the approach; GEO results also switch the orbit display to ECF so the relative motion is easy to read. The download button exports the list as a CSV file.
help.howToUse:
1. Open the menu from the bottom bar or press X.
  2. Choose the Orbit Type (Geostationary or Low Earth Orbit) and set the Max Distance Threshold, Max Relative Velocity, and Search Duration.
  3. Enter a NORAD ID, or check Geostationary All vs All for a belt wide survey.
  4. Click Find RPOs. Long searches show progress; results open in the list panel.
  5. Click any row to fly to that encounter.
help.tip1: A low relative velocity at close range is what distinguishes deliberate proximity operations from a fast conjunction flyby; tighten the velocity threshold to filter for maneuvers.
help.tip2: All vs all GEO searches are heavy; narrow the duration first, then expand once the thresholds look right.
help.tip3: Clicking a GEO result switches orbits to Earth fixed (ECF) display; the toast tells you which mode is active.
